title: Polystichum discretum
intro: Polystichum discretum, also known as the Japanese holly fern or Japanese wood fern, is a species of fern belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant is prized for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and is traditionally used to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Polystichum discretum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Japanese medicine, Polystichum discretum is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being incorporated into herbal preparations such as tinctures and salves.
taxonomy: Polystichum discretum is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a member of the genus Polystichum, which includes over 260 species of evergreen, terrestrial, or epiphytic ferns. The genus Polystichum is part of the larger group Polypodiidae, which is a suborder of ferns within the class Polypodiopsida. The class Polypodiopsida is a division of vascular plants that also includes the class Ophioglossidae. Polystichum discretum's classification places it within the kingdom Plantae.
